auto_approve.mechanism: Cursor does not document pre-configured auto-approval for specific MCP tools.

enterprise_management.mechanism: Cursor does not document organization-level managed MCP policy controls.

env_var_expansion.mechanism: Server env blocks reference environment variables so secrets can be read from the shell environment rather than hardcoded.

marketplace.mechanism: Cursor ships an in-IDE MCP directory for discovering and installing community MCP servers.

oauth_support.mechanism: Cursor's MCP documentation does not describe OAuth 2.0 flows for remote servers.

resource_referencing.mechanism: Cursor's MCP documentation does not describe referencing MCP resources by URI.

tool_filtering.mechanism: Cursor does not document per-server tool allowlists or blocklists for MCP servers.

transport_types.mechanism: Cursor documents three MCP transports: stdio for local processes, sse for Server-Sent Events, and streamable-http for HTTP-based remote servers.

providerExtensions.name: Three MCP transport mechanisms
providerExtensions.summary: Supports stdio, sse, and streamable-http transports chosen by the server configuration.

providerExtensions.name: Project and global MCP config files
providerExtensions.summary: Project-scope servers live in .cursor/mcp.json at the repo root; user-global servers live in ~/.cursor/mcp.json.

providerExtensions.name: Environment variables in server env blocks
providerExtensions.summary: Per-server env maps pass environment variables (including secrets sourced from the user's shell) to stdio servers.

providerExtensions.name: In-IDE MCP directory
providerExtensions.summary: Cursor exposes an MCP directory inside the IDE for browsing and installing community MCP servers without editing mcp.json by hand.
